Tests that dividing negative infinity by positive infinity results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the result of dividing `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY` by `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` in JavaScript. The expression `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY / Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` is expected to evaluate to `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `isNaN` function is used to verify this. The assertion ensures that the result of the division is indeed `NaN` by comparing it to `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on validating a specific mathematical operation involving infinite values in JavaScript.
